title: rubus fruticosus subsp. caesius
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. caesius, also known as blue-black raspberry or wild blue raspberry, is a subspecies of the blackberry plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus fruticosus subsp. caesius include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can be used to support overall health and well-being.
